storyInfo: Wang encounters a weiqi maniac and recognizes Sui's trap in the game. He uses the writing knife to scrape away every 'Sui' character in the book until he dies. This round, he sacrifices three black stones to kill Sui in the dream, disrupting Sui's memory.

attributes.text: Better to sacrifice a few stones... than to lose the initiative...
One wrong move... and all is lost!

attributes.content: ...So you found me.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: You've been following me for quite a while. Show yourself.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Wang looks back. The mixture of weeping and lament moves away gradually, but it does not stop for even an instant.
Hmph.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Let me see what you've got.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Wang follows the eerie voice to a decrepit weiqi stall.
A man, presumably the owner of the stall, sits on the ground weeping. There is no one around.
One wrong move... and all is lost...
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: And all is lost!
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: Before Wang has a chance to take in the situation on the weiqi board, the man falls upon it and begins to wail, sending stones flying.
Ridiculous.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: As Wang turns around to leave, the man's crying begins to abate.
Don't go, sir, please, I'll set up the board again...
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: This game cost me my life savings, my home, my farm, even my family scattered and some of them died because of it... I am very sick and don't have long to live.
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: A game. A game of weiqi, that cost me my home, my family, my life...
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: I couldn't let it go. Take a look for me. If you can find a solution...
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: ...Perhaps fate can still be changed!
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: The man goes from sobbing to howling. As Wang stands motionless, the man picks up the stones from the ground, and quickly sets up the board.
Setup, probe, entrap, sacrifice, fight, exchange...

The man's hands move at a breathtaking pace. The game begins to grow as though it is alive, the war raging from the corners to the center, then to the other side which still lies empty, so fast the eyes can hardly keep up.
Before Wang can react, the man has put a black stone in his hand.
Your turn, sir.
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: ...How?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Yes. How, indeed?
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: Black and white should have taken turns placing stones. But no black stone can be seen on the board, once you hold one. How should the game proceed?
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: This is ridiculous... I'll take my leave.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: You ought to understand, sir.
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: This board is like an abyss. Every white stone is the size of a mountain, while the black ones that I have presumptuously placed upon it... are no more than game pieces.
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: We are so far from the board that even the mountains are no greater than the size of a thumb. How could we possibly see a tiny weiqi stone?
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: I held on as long as I could, but at this point I was dazed and exhausted. One wrong move...
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: The man begins to weep bitterly again—
...And all is lost!
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: Look! I gave each stone a name, but I couldn't save any of them! There is no way out for any of them!
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: This one is Shuo. This one is Wang. This one is Ling. This one is Jun—
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: Wang draws his blade and stabs at one of the white stones, only for it to make a sharp cracking sound as it bends, as though he were stabbing a boulder.
Pointless, like striking a rock with an egg...
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: Why did I play this game? I shouldn't have. Why...?
attributes.name: Strange Man

attributes.content: The sword turns and pierces the man's throat. He slumps to the ground, but no blood flows from the wound. The weeping grows even louder, though it is no longer intelligible.
The man's eyes glow with a strange expression. Is it fear or joy? He stares straight at Wang's face.
Wang instinctively reaches for the writing knife on his belt, but stops mid-way.
Finally, he ponders for a moment and puts the black stone in his hand on the board.
All is lost? Nonsense.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Set it here, and the game remains a toss-up.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: No sooner have the words left Wang's mouth than the body on the ground turns into smoke, and the invisible black stones on the board reveal themselves.
Mr. Wang!
attributes.name: Patrolling Candleholder

attributes.content: You...
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: I watched you stand in front of the door to the Sui Regulator before turning around again. I came to find you as I thought you might have met with a strange happening too.
attributes.name: Patrolling Candleholder

attributes.content: Strange happening? You already know?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Yes.
attributes.name: Patrolling Candleholder

attributes.content: ...I suppose I shouldn't be surprised.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: There was a madman here crying about some omen of misfortune. He didn't make any sense.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Where is he?
attributes.name: Patrolling Candleholder

attributes.content: Vanished. His weiqi game remains.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Understood. I'll have someone copy it down.
attributes.name: Patrolling Candleholder

attributes.content: One more thing. The Supervisor says Jie should be about done with the book copying, and that you probably brought the copies. Have you...?
attributes.name: Patrolling Candleholder

attributes.content: Here.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: One moment please while I count them.
attributes.name: Patrolling Candleholder

attributes.content: Three poetry anthologies for a total of thirty-two scrolls...
attributes.name: Patrolling Candleholder

attributes.content: Five volumes on philology for a total of sixty scrolls...
attributes.name: Patrolling Candleholder

attributes.content: One volume on divination for a total of five scrolls...
attributes.name: Patrolling Candleholder

attributes.content: This is... the Jijiuzhang? Looks like it got mixed in with the divination books.
attributes.name: Patrolling Candleholder

attributes.content: Jie is such a genius. She wrote this in a single night, all one thousand and four hundred words of it, without a single character repeated.
attributes.name: Patrolling Candleholder

attributes.content: Her calligraphy is still so beautiful. There is truly no equal.
attributes.name: Patrolling Candleholder

attributes.content: The Candleholder heaps praise upon the scroll. Wang follows his gaze—
And sees an empty piece of paper.
I remember this... five hundred years ago, in the capital.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: The first time she proposed an actionable hypothesis for destroying Sui.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: She gave me the writing knife, but I don't remember what the article was about, what she wanted to tell me...
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Hah. By 'repeat', did you mean making me repeat the events of the past in dream?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.text: Only to make you see for yourself how many fantasies have appeared in your dreams over the past thousand years, dreams about destroying me, dreams that were doomed to fail.
I should turn these memories into a cage, and drown you in the shame of countless failures.
See, now... all the memories that you have forgotten.

attributes.content: You forgot?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Sorry.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: It's nothing. I'm just thinking too much, if the Sui Regulator didn't say anything...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I would like to have my writing knife back.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: *sigh*... I don't know why you always seem so aloof. We don't see each other very often. Are you not happy at all?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I wonder if you'd regret it, if one of us suddenly disappeared forever.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I'd rather not be called 'the Fifth Sui' my entire life. As if Its mark were still on me.
The ones who create characters helped me think of one to use as a name. Could you read this for me, brother?
I shall write it and recite it for you.
The characters '■' and '■', '■' and '■', come together to form '■'.
Mr. Wang?
attributes.name: Patrolling Candleholder

attributes.content: Is something the matter?
attributes.name: Patrolling Candleholder

attributes.content: I remembered something.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: My sister said she did the Jijiuzhang in a hurry and asked me to check it for errors. I forgot.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Give me some time.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Not a problem.
attributes.name: Patrolling Candleholder

attributes.content: I'll leave this one for now, then. It's getting late. You can bring it back tomorrow.
attributes.name: Patrolling Candleholder

attributes.content: A cage of memories?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Ridiculous... You won't get your wish.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: The sky has completely darkened by the time the proxy arrives at the inn.
'Ji Jiu Qi Gu Yu Zhong Yi, Luo Lie Zhu Wu Ming Xing Zi.' The Jijiuzhang is distinctive, unlike other books: it lists the names of many things and persons.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: ...I should have remembered.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: A breeze turns the pages. Wang seems to see a piece of paper in a different color on the back cover.
She was then trying to tell me something...
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Wang picks up the thin booklet from the table and opens it.

attributes.text: 'Ji Jiu Qi Gu Yu Zhong Yi, Luo Lie Zhu Wu Ming Xing Zi.' The Jijiuzhang is distinctive, unlike other books: it lists the names of many things and persons.\n\n'Fen Bie Bu Ju Bu Za Ce, Yong Ri Yue Shao Cheng Kuai Yi, Mian Li Wu Zhi Bi You Xi.' They are classified into different sections by kind, so that nothing is confused or mixed together. With but little time expended, it suffices for daily use—truly to one's satisfaction. If one studies it with diligence, one is sure to taste the delight of success.

attributes.content: These should have been the words written on the first page. These were the words, when Wang scanned the page for the first time.
But now, the paper is covered in strange ink markings.

attributes.text: <color=#d70000>Sui Sui Sui Sui Sui Sui Sui, Sui Sui Sui Sui Sui Sui Sui\n\nSui Sui Sui Sui Sui Sui Sui, Sui Sui Sui Sui Sui Sui Sui, Sui Sui Sui Sui Sui Sui Sui</color>

attributes.content: Wang instinctively raises the writing knife and aims it at the first fierce-looking 'Sui' on the page.
One wrong move...
Who's there?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: And all is lost...
The wind howls outside the window, but there is no one there.
Still trying to be our nightmare?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: The same old tricks from centuries ago. Have you not grown tired of it yet?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: When at a disadvantage, seek a draw...
There is no such thing as a draw between us.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: When in danger, make sacrifices...
In your dreams.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: The writing knife strikes the first 'Sui'.
Wang has used his sister's writing knife before. A slight cut, and the incorrect word is completely erased, leaving the page intact.
But he feels a great resistance, as though he is trying to scrape a boulder, a mountain even.
He tightens his not-too-strong arms and pushes down—
A crack of thunder outside the window.
The first 'Sui' finally disappears. The paper is blank for a moment, then the word that Jie wrote is revealed, the lines intentionally plain yet vigorous to make for easier recognition by learners.
The lone 'Ji' stands eerie amongst a page full of 'Sui'.
Is that it?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Is smearing and dabbing the worst you can do?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Do not give in to haste...
Hmph.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Wang takes aim at the second 'Sui' and scrapes it. The resistance seems even stronger than the first time.
'Jiu.'
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: The night wind invades the room, trying to turn the pages, but Wang keeps the paper firmly under his palm.
'Qi.'
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: 'Gu'—
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: The room starts to shake violently as the fourth word reveals itself.
The thunder outside rumbles endlessly, accompanied by constant lightning.
'Zhong'...
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: A bolt of lightning strikes the inn, setting it ablaze.
The room burns fiercely, but Wang remains at the desk, utterly focused on cutting the page.
Each 'Sui' requires greater exertion than the last. Each time a 'Sui' is erased, a different hellish vision appears around him.
The sky sunders, the earth trembles, winds shriek and thunder roars, and fires burn red and bright.
The beams, the bed, the desk, the shelves... until there is nothing in the room left to burn, and even the ground has been burned through.
Wang can feel himself drop straight down like a stone, but his hands do not stop.
Scorching heat.
Freezing cold.
Suffocation.
Isolation...
One hundred, three hundred, five hundred, one thousand...
One thousand three hundred and ninety-three...
One thousand three hundred and ninety-four...
The last 'Sui' is erased, and he finds himself in the dark room again.

Nothing in the room has changed, except for Wang sitting in the middle of the room, his face gaunt and yellow, his clothes tattered, as though he physically journeyed through all these hellish visions.
(Heavy breathing)
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: What other weiqi wisdom do you have...? Spit it out, won't you?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Silence.
Wang turns the page. There is indeed a paper between the last page and the back cover. It is not words that are written on it, but a nineteen-by-nineteen weiqi board drawn with writing ink.
Not a classic match, but a handicap game of clearly mismatched sides.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Wait, this is...
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.text: It's one thing to make me play weiqi with you because you're bored, dear brother, it's another to use the stones to form the character for 'bored.'
As if that would help me against you.
Why don't you find something else to pass the time, if it's not really a weiqi game that you're after? Go hiking, learn an instrument... I could teach you calligraphy, if it comes down to that.
Oh? What thoughts did you read this time?

attributes.content: There are words on the other side...
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Through the paper, Wang can see that the writing is different from the simple, straightforward lines that she used when copying for learners; it is the unique style of calligraphy that she uses when writing to her siblings.
He flips the paper over, trying to see what the message he never managed to see was—
But every word is covered by the 'Sui' character, even thicker and fiercer than the ones that he has just erased.
Same old tricks? Fine. Let's see what tricks you've got, other than illusions and parlor tricks—
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Once again, Wang stabs the 'Sui' covering the page with the writing knife, wiping away a little smear on the top-right of the word...
No unusual phenomenon occurs, except the red blood that begins to flow from his right shoulder.
Urgh?!
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: To kill you is to kill me... is that so?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Suits me just fine...!
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: One stab, and the wound on his shoulder opens up.
Another stab, and the wound deepens to reach the bone.
Another stab.
And another...
Missing words? Let's see... what's the word... I can't remember...
attributes.name: Bookseller's Voice

attributes.content: In October, I'll be nine... nine...
attributes.name: Child's Voice

attributes.content: Supervisor! Urgent report from the Sky Mirror Pavilion. All records pertaining to the Feranmut are—
attributes.name: Candleholder's Voice

attributes.content: Ugh... argh!
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Perhaps the blood has gotten into his eyes, for it seems like the 'Sui' character covering the page has begun to move, as though it is alive.
The ink seems to fear the writing knife, shifting back and forth among the names of the twelve proxies...
It stops on the 'Wang' character.
Wang pauses and presses his bloody right side with his left hand, then resolutely points the writing knife back at the word that covers everything.
The ink is so black it seems to shine, reflecting half of Wang's silhouette.
...The last cut.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: The writing knife glows bright. The proxy pours his entire spirit into it, waiting for it to strike his own name...
Haah—!
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: The ink on the page dissipates completely, revealing the beautiful calligraphy beneath.
But the proxy can see it no more.
He falls to the ground, covered in blood, so thin and gaunt he hardly seems human.
He has stopped breathing.

attributes.text: I wanted to kill myself. I have gotten my wish.
After more than a century of plotting since your last failed attempt, this is how it ends, I the strategist, I the schemer?

attributes.content: You're delusional.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: The proxy stands beside his bloody corpse on the floor, perfectly unharmed as he was when he entered the Tomb of Sui.
The body on the ground dissipates like the morning dew, until only three shining black stones are left—
Until they, too, disintegrate.

attributes.text: Turning yourself into weiqi stones. This was your idea.

attributes.content: Three black stones in exchange for killing you once. A fair trade.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Fooling myself?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: I've visited many of your kind. I know how incomprehensible you are... how incomprehensible we are.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: I know about the faint consciousnesses that were born from the primordial void, and I know how they have transformed themselves into colossal entities in the course of an infinity.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Time, space, quantity, structure... the things that mortals take for granted, the things that seem to be everlasting, unchangeable, are merely a temporary roost for you.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: I have seen one of your kind continue to disrupt what should have been the calm flow of time, even though it had died long ago and been turned into a machine at the hands of others.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.text: And you still dream of killing me?

attributes.content: Perhaps you have no need to die. But you will fall into eternal slumber, Sui, the dreamer of evolution.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Mankind has no use for your arrogant design, and I have no use for your endless dream.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Every death in your memory is real, a genuine death of a small part of your enormous consciousness.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Don't believe me? Think back to the day I encountered that 'madman', back when your methods were so primitive they were downright laughable. What did I do?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Did I give the book back to the Sui Regulator? Did I destroy it? What was written in it? Was it really a book?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: See? You've forgotten.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Your memory has died, the price you paid in exchange for three black stones.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: To be precise, you died by the writing knife imbued with a proxy's entire life force.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: He scraped clean every instance of the 'Sui' character across all Great Yan, and with it any trace that a creature called 'Sui' ever existed.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: I would do it again, had I the writing knife in my hand right now.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: I would be happy to create for you a nightmare, from which you would never wake. Even at the cost of my own life.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.text: The more parts of me you kill, the more you think you have weakened me, and the sooner I will wake.

attributes.content: I know.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Death hurts. Each and every one of your memories is real. They are happening somewhere. The pain of your death cuts straight to the heart.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.text: What about you? What can you do?

attributes.content: I will continue the game.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Until neither of us can ever rise again.
attributes.name: Wang
